what_it_does: Records a raised 'signal' in the channel-memory noticeboard that the global visual-fidelity decision has been decided, selecting the option 'Push the drawn style further first', and that work gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity may resume.
observations: YAML frontmatter declares id, board:// address, type=signal, source=claude_code, state=raised, author_session=operator
A single link of kind attached_to points to decision://global/visual-fidelity
History shows one transition from null to raised by operator with note 'filed'
Body text restates the decision, chosen option, and notes that resuming is the lane's/operator's gated step
standouts: Decision target decision://global/visual-fidelity is marked resolved with a chosen option
Chosen option is 'Push the drawn style further first'
State is raised, making it an active notice rather than a draft or closed record
conventions: Follows the ledger item schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, links, timestamps, and history
Uses a board:// URI as the item address
Uses a decision:// URI for the linked decision
Signal type and 'decided' wording match a decision-notification convention
notes: A decision-resolution signal that unblocks visual-fidelity work by committing to the drawn-style-first option.
questions: Which specific lanes or tasks are gated on decision://global/visual-fidelity?
What does 'drawn style' mean in this project's visual context?
What alternative options were considered and rejected?
Who is responsible for executing the resume step?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the file's stated purpose is to record the decided visual-fidelity option and unblock gated work, and that is exactly what it contains.
apparent_themes: Decision gating and explicit unblock signals
intention_signals: To communicate that the visual-fidelity choice is finalized and to authorize downstream work to proceed along the 'drawn style further first' path.
contribution: A canonical, timestamped record of the selected visual-fidelity option and the signal that dependent work is no longer blocked by that decision.
summary_for_embedding: A raised noticeboard signal recording the decision to push the drawn style further first for the global visual-fidelity decision and stating that gated work may resume.
intention_for_embedding: To notify the system that the visual-fidelity decision is settled so that gated lanes can resume with the chosen drawn-style-first option.
